texto: Americans' distrust of the conspicuously intellectual - a habit we learned, I suppose, on the frontier, but which remains a feature of the national character - has the virtue of puncturing the pretentious and exposing the fake, but it may also have impaired American listeners' patience for music that is especially complex or austere.
explicacao: Citação de Steven Stucky sobre temas ligados a sabedoria. “Americans' distrust of the conspicuously intellectual - a habit we learned, I suppose, on the fro…” articula sabedoria, music, character em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como character e american.

frase_original: Americans' distrust of the conspicuously intellectual - a habit we learned, I suppose, on the frontier, but which remains a feature of the national character - has the virtue of puncturing the pretentious and exposing the fake, but it may also have impaired American listeners' patience for music that is especially complex or austere.
